title: resolve SEALED placeholders + URL-encode admin password
description:
## Summary

- **Bug 1 — URL encoding**: The admin token request used `-d "...&password=${KC_ADMIN_PASS}"`. The `&` character in the sealed admin password was treated as a URL form-field separator by curl, sending a truncated password and always failing to obtain a token (`ENV=korczewski` has been silently broken since the secrets were sealed with this password).
- **Bug 2 — SEALED placeholder**: `env-resolve.sh` exports `KC_USER1_PASSWORD=SEALED` for prod environments (that's the YAML placeholder, not the real value). The script was setting Keycloak user passwords to the literal string `"SEALED"`. Added `_resolve_sealed()` / `_secret_val()` helpers that fetch the actual value from the live `workspace-secrets` Secret when the YAML value is the `SEALED` placeholder — the same pattern already used for `KC_ADMIN_PASS`.

Root cause discovered while debugging why nobody on korczewski could log in after tonight's Keycloak restart. Passwords were reset manually via `kcadm.sh`; this fix makes `task workspace:admin-users-setup ENV=korczewski` idempotent and self-healing going forward.

## Test plan

- [x] `task workspace:admin-users-setup ENV=korczewski` completes successfully (paddione + gekko passwords updated)
- [x] Direct grant token test: `paddione` and `gekko` both authenticate successfully against `workspace` realm
- [x] `task workspace:admin-users-setup` (dev, no ENV) still works — SEALED branch not taken when value is a real password

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
